blurb: Bigelow's tansyaster throws big lavender-purple, gold-eyed daisies on branching stems from late summer into fall, when pollinators need them most. A short-lived, self-sowing native of dry, sunny ground that drifts politely through a xeric bed.
spread: Clumping and short-lived (biennial to short-lived perennial); self-sows to persist
seasons: Grayish foliage; big lavender-purple, gold-eyed daisies from late summer into fall
wildlife: Important late-season nectar for native bees and butterflies; a specialist-bee (tansyaster) host
